lesson.subtitle: Aula 6 -- Sports and Handball -- Live Action vs Routine
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carolina Paludetto Rodrigues | Aula 6 -- Sports and Handball

listenings.text: Good afternoon, everyone, and welcome to the handball final here at the sports center. The two teams are walking onto the court right now, and the crowd is cheering. The match starts in just a moment. Remember, a handball team has seven players, and they play two halves of thirty minutes. And here we go! Carolina has the ball. She is running fast down the court. She passes to a teammate, she gets the ball back, and she is throwing it now. She scores! What a player! Her team is winning by two points. The coach is shouting instructions from the side. Normally this team defends very well, and right now they are defending again. The other team is trying hard, but Carolina is too quick today. The crowd is on its feet. This is a wonderful match, and there is still ten minutes to play.

Hi, this is Carolina. You asked me about handball, so here is my week. I play handball because I love it. My team trains twice a week, on Tuesdays and Thursdays, after school. On Saturdays we usually have a match. I always wake up early on game day, and I never eat too much before a game. On the court, I am normally a fast player, and I like to pass the ball and to score. Right now I am sitting at home and I am watching a video of our last match, so I can learn from it. In the video, the coach is explaining a new move, and my team is defending really well. We do not win every time, but we improve every week. Next Saturday we have an important match, and I think we are ready to win. Come and watch us play!

extra_audio.text: The two teams walk onto the court and the match starts. Carolina gets the ball and runs fast down the court. She passes to a teammate and gets the ball back. She throws the ball and scores a point. Her team defends well and wins the match.
